title: OpenSSL HollowByte: A DoS Hiding in 11 Bytes
excerpt: Every so often, a vulnerability reminds us how deeply our digital infrastructure relies on foundational libraries. Recently, the Okta Red Team discovered
content_html:
Every so often, a vulnerability reminds us how deeply our digital infrastructure relies on foundational libraries. Recently, the Okta Red Team discovered HollowByte, a Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability in OpenSSL. By sending a malicious payload of just 11 bytes, a remote, unauthenticated attacker can force a server to allocate disproportionate chunks of memory before any security handshake even begins.

Here is the breakdown of how it works.

Trusting the Header

The TLS handshake begins with a ClientHello message wrapped in a record. Each handshake message carries a 4-byte header that declares how large the incoming message body will be.

Older versions of OpenSSL allocate a receive buffer based on that attacker-declared length before any data has actually arrived.

When a rogue header lands, the state machine triggers an unvalidated allocation. When the malicious 11-byte payload arrives, the TLS state machine reads the 4-byte handshake header and triggers an unvalidated pre-allocation based on the header's 3-byte length declaration:

Read Header⟶grow_init_buf()⟶OPENSSL_clear_realloc()⟶malloc(attacker_size)

Because there is no payload validation at this early stage, malloc() allocates up to 131 KB based solely on the untrusted packet's claims. The worker thread then blocks, waiting indefinitely for data that will never arrive.

Permanent Memory Fragmentation

Holding connections open to exhaust threads is a classic trick (like Slowloris). HollowByte introduces a nastier compounding effect due to how the GNU C Library (glibc) handles memory.

When an attacking connection drops, OpenSSL frees the buffer. However, glibc does not immediately return small-to-medium allocations to the operating system; it keeps them for potential reuse.

By launching waves of connections with randomized claimed sizes, an attacker prevents the allocator from reusing those freed chunks. The heap fragments heavily, causing the server’s Resident Set Size (RSS) to climb continuously.

Even after the attacker disconnects, the server remains permanently bloated. The only way to reclaim that memory is to terminate the process.

Real-World Testing & Ecosystem Impact

To measure the threat, we tested unpatched and patched OpenSSL instances running NGINX under various load conditions.

In a standard 1 GB RAM environment, the unpatched server was OOM-killed at 547 MB of frozen, fragmented memory. In higher-spec testing (16 GB RAM), the attack successfully locked up 25% of the system's total memory while staying safely under the connection ceiling, meaning standard connection-limiting defenses won't stop it.

Because OpenSSL is widely used and embedded, this vulnerability affects a variety of software, including web servers (Apache, NGINX), language runtimes (Node.js, Python, Ruby, PHP), and databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL).

The Fix and Next Steps

The OpenSSL team resolved this by moving to incremental buffer growth (merged in PRs #30792, #30793, and #30794). This fix was silently included as part of the OpenSSL v4.0.1 release, with silent backports to release versions 3.6.3, 3.5.7, 3.4.6, and 3.0.21. Instead of trusting the header outright, OpenSSL now grows the buffer only as bytes actually land on the wire. A claim with no follow-through now costs the server nothing.

While OpenSSL handled this as a hardening fix rather than a CVE security advisory, we recommend upgrading your distribution's OpenSSL packages immediately.

By sending a malicious payload of just 11 bytes, a remote, unauthenticated attacker can force a server to allocate disproportionate chunks of memory before any security handshake even begins.

sentences.en: Every so often, a vulnerability reminds us how deeply our digital infrastructure relies on foundational libraries.

sentences.zh: 当攻击连接断开时，OpenSSL 会释放缓冲区。

sentences.en: However, glibc does not immediately return small-to-medium allocations to the operating system; it keeps them for potential reuse.
sentences.zh: 但是，glibc 不会立即将中小型分配返回给操作系统；它保留它们以供潜在的重复使用。

sentences.zh: 通过发起具有随机声明大小的连接波，攻击者可以阻止分配器重用这些已释放的块。

sentences.en: The heap fragments heavily, causing the server’s Resident Set Size (RSS) to climb continuously.
sentences.zh: 堆碎片严重，导致服务器的驻留集大小（RSS）不断攀升。

blocks.html_en: Even after the attacker disconnects, the server remains permanently bloated. The only way to reclaim that memory is to terminate the process.

sentences.en: Even after the attacker disconnects, the server remains permanently bloated.
sentences.zh: 即使攻击者断开连接后，服务器仍然会永久膨胀。

sentences.en: The only way to reclaim that memory is to terminate the process.
sentences.zh: 回收内存的唯一方法是终止进程。

blocks.html_en: To measure the threat, we tested unpatched and patched OpenSSL instances running NGINX under various load conditions.

sentences.zh: 为了衡量威胁，我们测试了在各种负载条件下运行 NGINX 的未修补和修补的 OpenSSL 实例。

blocks.html_en: In a standard 1 GB RAM environment, the unpatched server was OOM-killed at 547 MB of frozen, fragmented memory. sentences.en: In a standard 1 GB RAM environment, the unpatched server was OOM-killed at 547 MB of frozen, fragmented memory.
sentences.zh: 在标准 1 GB RAM 环境中，未打补丁的服务器在 547 MB​​ 冻结的碎片内存中被 OOM 杀死。

sentences.en: In higher-spec testing (16 GB RAM), the attack successfully locked up 25% of the system's total memory while staying safely under the connection ceiling, meaning standard connection-limiting defenses won't stop it.
sentences.zh: 在更高规格的测试（16 GB RAM）中，攻击成功锁定了系统总内存的 25%，同时安全地保持在连接上限之下，这意味着标准的连接限制防御无法阻止它。

blocks.html_en: Because OpenSSL is widely used and embedded, this vulnerability affects a variety of software, including web servers (Apache, NGINX), language runtimes (Node.js, Python, Ruby, PHP), and databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL).

sentences.en: Because OpenSSL is widely used and embedded, this vulnerability affects a variety of software, including web servers (Apache, NGINX), language runtimes (Node.js, Python, Ruby, PHP), and databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL).
sentences.zh: 由于 OpenSSL 被广泛使用和嵌入，该漏洞影响多种软件，包括 Web 服务器（Apache、NGINX）、语言运行时（Node.js、Python、Ruby、PHP）和数据库（MySQL、PostgreSQL）。

blocks.html_en: The Fix and Next Steps

sentences.en: The Fix and Next Steps
sentences.zh: 修复和后续步骤

blocks.html_en: The OpenSSL team resolved this by moving to incremental buffer growth (merged in PRs #30792, #30793, and #30794). This fix was silently included as part of the OpenSSL v4.0.1 release, with silent backports to release versions 3.6.3, 3.5.7, 3.4.6, and 3.0.21. Instead of trusting the header outright, OpenSSL now grows the buffer only as bytes actually land on the wire. A claim with no follow-through now costs the server nothing.

sentences.en: The OpenSSL team resolved this by moving to incremental buffer growth (merged in PRs #30792, #30793, and #30794).
sentences.zh: OpenSSL 团队通过转向增量缓冲区增长解决了这个问题（合并在 PR #30792、#30793 和 #30794 中）。

sentences.en: This fix was silently included as part of the OpenSSL v4.0.1 release, with silent backports to release versions 3.6.3, 3.5.7, 3.4.6, and 3.0.21.
sentences.zh: 此修复已静默包含在 OpenSSL v4.0.1 版本中，并静默向后移植到版本 3.6.3、3.5.7、3.4.6 和 3.0.21。

sentences.en: Instead of trusting the header outright, OpenSSL now grows the buffer only as bytes actually land on the wire.
sentences.zh: OpenSSL 现在不再完全信任标头，而是仅在字节实际到达线路时才增加缓冲区。

sentences.en: A claim with no follow-through now costs the server nothing.
sentences.zh: 现在，没有后续处理的索赔不会给服务器带来任何成本。

blocks.html_en: While OpenSSL handled this as a hardening fix rather than a CVE security advisory, we recommend upgrading your distribution's OpenSSL packages immediately.

sentences.en: While OpenSSL handled this as a hardening fix rather than a CVE security advisory, we recommend upgrading your distribution's OpenSSL packages immediately.
sentences.zh: 虽然 OpenSSL 将此作为强化修复而不是 CVE 安全公告来处理，但我们建议立即升级您的发行版的 OpenSSL 软件包。

content_html_zh:
漏洞时常提醒我们，我们的数字基础设施对基础库的依赖程度有多深。 最近，Okta Red Team 发现了 HollowByte，这是 OpenSSL 中的拒绝服务 (DoS) 漏洞。 通过发送仅 11 字节的恶意负载，未经身份验证的远程攻击者可以强制服务器在任何安全握手开始之前分配不成比例的内存块。

以下是其工作原理的详细说明。

信任标题

TLS 握手以封装在记录中的 ClientHello 消息开始。 每个握手消息都带有一个 4 字节的标头，用于声明传入消息正文的大小。

旧版本的 OpenSSL 在任何数据实际到达之前根据攻击者声明的长度分配接收缓冲区。

当恶意标头到达时，状态机会触发未经验证的分配。 当恶意 11 字节有效负载到达时，TLS 状态机读取 4 字节握手标头，并根据标头的 3 字节长度声明触发未经验证的预分配：

读头⟶grow_init_buf()⟶OPENSSL_clear_realloc()⟶malloc(attacker_size)

由于在此早期阶段没有有效负载验证，因此 malloc() 仅根据不受信任的数据包的声明分配最多 131 KB。 然后工作线程会阻塞，无限期地等待永远不会到达的数据。

永久内存碎片

保持与排气线程的连接打开是一个经典技巧（如 Slowloris）。 由于 GNU C 库 (glibc) 处理内存的方式，HollowByte 引入了更严重的复合效应。

当攻击连接断开时，OpenSSL 会释放缓冲区。 但是，glibc 不会立即将中小型分配返回给操作系统；它保留它们以供潜在的重复使用。

通过发起具有随机声明大小的连接波，攻击者可以阻止分配器重用这些已释放的块。 堆碎片严重，导致服务器的驻留集大小（RSS）不断攀升。

即使攻击者断开连接后，服务器仍然会永久膨胀。 回收内存的唯一方法是终止进程。

真实世界测试和生态系统影响

为了衡量威胁，我们测试了在各种负载条件下运行 NGINX 的未修补和修补的 OpenSSL 实例。

在标准 1 GB RAM 环境中，未打补丁的服务器在 547 MB​​ 冻结的碎片内存中被 OOM 杀死。 在更高规格的测试（16 GB RAM）中，攻击成功锁定了系统总内存的 25%，同时安全地保持在连接上限之下，这意味着标准的连接限制防御无法阻止它。

由于 OpenSSL 被广泛使用和嵌入，该漏洞影响多种软件，包括 Web 服务器（Apache、NGINX）、语言运行时（Node.js、Python、Ruby、PHP）和数据库（MySQL、PostgreSQL）。

修复和后续步骤

OpenSSL 团队通过转向增量缓冲区增长解决了这个问题（合并在 PR #30792、#30793 和 #30794 中）。 此修复已静默包含在 OpenSSL v4.0.1 版本中，并静默向后移植到版本 3.6.3、3.5.7、3.4.6 和 3.0.21。 OpenSSL 现在不再完全信任标头，而是仅在字节实际到达线路时才增加缓冲区。 现在，没有后续处理的索赔不会给服务器带来任何成本。

虽然 OpenSSL 将此作为强化修复而不是 CVE 安全公告来处理，但我们建议立即升级您的发行版的 OpenSSL 软件包。
